hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'vanitas' with genitive 'vanitatis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'vanitas' with -atis genitive is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei — 'vanitas' has genitive 'vanitatis'

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'vanitates' — 'vanitatum' with -um ending is genitive plural
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural would be 'vanitates' — 'vanitatum' is genitive plural

hints.Ablative of means: Ablative of means uses ablative case — 'vanitatum' is genitive, forming a Hebrew superlative
hints.Dative of reference: Dative of reference uses dative case — 'vanitatum' is genitive plural
hints.Ablative absolute: Ablative absolute requires a participle and noun in ablative — this is a genitive construction

hints.Accusative: 'omnia' could be accusative neuter plural, but here it's the subject of the implied 'est/sunt'

steps.prompt: What verb is implied in this sentence?

hints.habet (has): The sentence equates 'omnia' with 'vanitas' — the copula 'est/sunt' is implied
hints.facit (makes): The sentence is a statement of identity, not action — 'est' is the implied copula
hints.venit (comes): This is an equation: all things = vanity — requiring the copula 'est'

hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'vanitatis' — 'vanitas' is predicate nominative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'vanitatem' — 'vanitas' is a predicate nominative

translation: Vanity of vanities, all is vanity
